Jesus died on the cross.  I  have a prob shitposting myself at times so i get it. I just hope alma doesnt get fed up with the bs from ppl who will fade into dark after a week or so anyways (or me for being impulsive responding) .  because out of all the weekly letters/emails i get from jpmorgan/schwabb/ect... comment.body: Cem’s had plenty of bad calls too. Dude buys his analysis from some cboe quant guys, fyi. Btw Alma called stagflation three years ago, long before Cem or anybody else did. He can’t trade, and half the time his insights are borderline indecipherable. He’s even been caught ripping off other people’s work, i.e. VolSig and a bunch of other quants I won’t name. Funny enough, he follows Alma too. Don’t forget, she posts her real-time trades..stuff you just can’t fake.. You used to see her pnl on her X profile, but she stopped sharing..that’s her personal business. What really matters is your pnl. I only spotted four Alma trades that ended up with small losses..i even engineered them to test it. And last year’s performance on X was very spot on

comment.body: The thing is, nobody will get everything right. People need confirmation from others and that's fine. I like signals but the ones i get I will always analyse before (but mostly after I shoot) :p One's goal should be to build a set of strategies, and learn from others to get better navigating in the jungle alone.  No conviction no long term success. Don't care about someone else's pnl, care about maximizing your own for the sake of living your best life with the people you love and the ones that need your help

comment.body: You know how easy it is to photoshop some unreal pnl? That proves zilch. Trading’s a mental game, and a lot of people just can’t hack it. Cem got burned selling short-dated vol last year, even though he was calling for a spot-up, vol-up move. He nailed the call, but he wasn’t mentally dialed in enough to actually stick to the play
